mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% and remains elevated for two consecutive months, equity risk premiums expand due to higher discount rates and reduced corporate profit margins. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 4.0% for 2+ consecutive months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion and consumer spending capacity. This drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations rise.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.10) during periods of sustained PAYEMS growth above 150k MoM, or correlation between PAYEMS growth and forward returns falls below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening consumer demand and construction activity. When both series decline simultaneously by more than 2% month-over-month, equity valuations compress due to reduced earnings visibility, producing negative 5-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines >2% MoM AND HOUST declines >2% MoM simultaneously
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of co-contraction events, or the joint signal has lower predictive power (Rank IC < 0.05) than either series alone over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above its 12-month trend signals expanding productive capacity and supply-side strength. This supports margin expansion and positive earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days in non-recessionary regimes.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during INDPRO growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, labor market deterioration signals reduced consumer confidence and future earnings headwinds. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as growth expectations decline.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) following UNRATE increases >0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) growth decelerates below 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains above 3.5%, the central bank enters a late-tightening phase with limited room for rate cuts. This creates uncertainty and negative 20-day forward returns as investors price in prolonged elevated rates.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances meeting both trigger conditions, or the interaction signal has lower Rank IC than FEDFUNDS alone over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter above 0.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion. This supports equity valuations and positive earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days in the quarter following the GDP release.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.10) during quarters with GDPC1 growth >0.5% annualized, or correlation falls below 0.12.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,000 thousand units while retail sales (RSAFS) remain flat or declining, the combination signals a demand-side shock affecting both residential and consumer spending. This produces negative 5-day forward returns as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST <1000k units AND RSAFS MoM growth ≤0%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of co-occurrence events, or the joint signal has Rank IC <0.05 compared to single-series signals over 2-year rolling windows.
